children.name: Python
children.description: The large pythons of Africa and Asia. Pythons are ambush predators that kill by constriction — tightening with each exhalation until the prey cannot breathe. They can swallow prey many times their head diameter by dislocating the lower jaws. The Reticulated Python is the world's longest snake.

children.name: Python reticulatus
children.description: World's longest snake, regularly exceeding 6 m and confirmed at over 7.5 m; stunning reticulated pattern provides camouflage in dappled forest light; has killed adult humans.

children.name: Python bivittatus
children.description: Massively invasive in the Florida Everglades following pet releases; has devastated small mammal populations; adult males have been documented killing white-tailed deer.

children.name: Python sebae
children.description: Africa's largest snake; ambushes prey at water; documented kills include goats, warthogs, and crocodiles.

children.name: Morelia spilota
children.description: Common and adaptable; thermoreceptive heat pits in labial scales; highly variable pattern across six subspecies; popular in captivity.

children.name: Morelia viridis
children.description: Vivid green; arboreal; loops itself over branches in a characteristic saddle posture; juveniles yellow or orange, shifting to green with age.

children.name: Liasis fuscus
children.description: Highly aquatic; feeds on freshwater mammals; aggregates in large numbers near Australian floodplains.

children.name: Antaresia childreni
children.description: One of the world's smallest pythons; roosts in caves; named after zoologist J.G. Children, not for suitability as children's pets.
